'Why don't you list crypto?'
Errr... we only list up-only assets...

Tl;dr starting yesterday, QFEX will not require document ID verification for its ordinary retail users. QFEX users have already been notified via email and Discord.
While we are all celebrating this as a MASSIVE UX improvement, the real reasons run deeper than that.
People don't talk about compliance because it's not sexy.
Everyone has a moral duty to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing. At QFEX, we take this duty seriously.
Long run, dexes whose main business model is facilitating crime will be shut down and their exec team sent to prison. Our commitment to compliance is the reason why we are able to attract the market makers and liquidity that we do.
For years, document KYC has been the standard method of enforcing compliance policies. In the age of AI, it has become painfully obvious to us (see the below video) that the bad guys will always be one step ahead of the compliance software providers. Like most illicit businesses, the economics are just in their favour.
A couple of months ago, we began looking for alternatives.
I'm delighted to announce the integration of a new, powerful AI-based tool that, for the first time, integrates user data across browser sessions, blockchain history and trade history across QFEX and all perp dexes to build a complete user profile and enforce compliance policies.
This mogs document KYC and allows us to rest in peace knowing that our users are genuine, without knowing their passport details.
How markets should be.

Quantum computing vs photonics computing
What is computing? A model that works well is
(i) represent some information in a physical system,
(ii) manipulate that system, and
(iii) read the state of the resultant system.
Computation is therefore governed by the type of physics it is using under the hood.
Classical computers use classical systems like transistors, which are governed by equations discovered centuries ago by the likes of Newton, Lagrange and Hamilton.
Quantum physics has an extra 'trick' called entanglement which enables a different type of parallelization. It's this feature which enables things like breaking RSA encryption via Shor's algorithm. But 'quantumizing' transistors into entangling qubits is tricky; useful hardware is many years away.
An exciting middle ground involves relaxing the idea of making a direct transistor analogue and using interfering systems of light waves to do computation instead ('photonics computing').
This approach is beginning to show promising speedups on important computational tasks, like the matrix multiplications which are pivotal to AI inference.
How to get exposure?
Before light can compute, it has to be generated, modulated, transmitted, routed, amplified, received and packaged. There are already a whole host of photonics companies that have specialized in this area for a long time, in other industry, and the hardware is well developed.

A message from our CEO, Annanay Kapila
@annanay
QFEX is the first 24/7 no-broker trading exchange for equities, commodities, and FX. On QFEX, traders have direct access to markets, thereby removing the need for costly and manipulative brokers. Futures on traditional exchanges have fixed expiries and settle at maturity. Our futures never expire. We leverage perpetual futures, or perps: contracts without expiration that use funding payments to stay in line with the spot market. QFEX is built by traders with traders in mind. Our team comprises of quants from Citadel, Jump, Tower, Barclays, and Flow. We're building the ultimate trading platform, bringing fairer markets to CFD traders, deeper liquidity to crypto traders, and perps to professional traders. The future of futures is QFEX.
QFEX will go live to the public in Q4 2025. Sign up for our waitlist now to get early access. Users will also receive volume-based referral rewards.

Perps for the People
Institutions have been trading perps long before crypto existed - through swaps.
In fact, you might own swaps yourself - many ETFs are just a wrapper for them, instead of the underlying basket of stocks (“synthetic replication”). The market for swaps is mind-bogglingly massive, but its off-exchange nature has many problems:
> heightened counterparty risk
> fragmented liquidity
> uncompetitive pricing
to name a few.
Crypto markets solved these problems years ago with perpetual futures. Let’s examine how perps on traditional assets revolutionize finance for both institutions and retail.

> Inflation Data (CPI, PCE): Consumer and producer prices, respectively, measure the Fed's ability to keep prices stable. Higher-than-expected inflation can signal a need for rate hikes.
> GDP (Gross Domestic Product): GDP indicates the overall health and growth trajectory of the economy, influencing the Fed's assessment of economic strength and potential policy responses.
> Retail Sales, Industrial Production and Consumer Confidence: Consumer spending is a huge part of U.S. GDP - these indicators provide key insights into overall economic sentiment.
3/

What is the Fed?
The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the United States, established as a direct response to a series of financial panics in the early 1900s:
the nation’s first set of diamond hands.
And just like our favorite group of diamond hands, the Fed has moderators: the “Federal Open Market Committee” or FOMC. They ensure the delivery of the "dual mandate” of maximum employment and price stability:
2/

1. Maximum employment means getting as many people employed as possible. Too many people in jobs makes salaries go up though, which can mess with price stability.
2. Price stability means targeting a 2% increase in the cost of living to the ‘average’ person, year on year. If you’ve heard of CPI, it's just measuring this with a ‘representative basket’ of goods, which apparently includes “fresh cakes and cupcakes”, “synthetic wigs” and golf club memberships…
Traders watch the Fed’s performance to assess what the Fed is likely to do next - more on that later.
3/
